description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Fukuoka Prefecture (福岡県, Fukuoka-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɸɯ̥.kɯꜜ.o.ka, -kɯ.o.kaꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Kyūshū.

aliases: True Essence of the Pure Land Teaching
description: school of Pure Land Buddhism founded by Shinran in japan; most widely practiced branch of Buddhism in Japan
1894–1895 war between the Qing dynasty and the Empire of Japan over influence in Joseon, fought chiefly in Joseon
description_wiki: The First Sino-Japanese War (25 July 1894 – 17 April 1895), or the First China–Japan War, was a conflict between the Qing dynasty of China and the Empire of Japan primarily for influence over Korea.

description: Japanese daimyo of the Sengoku period
description_wiki: Takeda Shingen (武田 信玄; December 1, 1521 – May 13, 1573) was a Japanese samurai and daimyō of the Sengoku period.

description: Japanese samurai and warlord (1534–1582)
description_wiki: Oda Nobunaga (織田 信長; [o.da (|) no.bɯ(ꜜ).na.ɡa, -na.ŋa] ; 23 June 1534 – 21 June 1582) was a Japanese samurai and daimyō and one of the leading figures of the Sengoku and Azuchi-Momoyama periods.

description: samurai who led the Western army in the Battle of Sekigahara
description_wiki: Ishida Mitsunari (石田 三成; Japanese pronunciation: [i.ɕi.da (|) mʲi.tsɯꜜ.na.ɾʲi], 1559 – November 6, 1600) was a Japanese samurai and military commander of the late Sengoku period of Japan.

description: samurai
description_wiki: Takayama Hikokurō (高山 彦九郎) (15 June 1747 – 4 August 1793) was a Japanese samurai and historian, and one of the early proponents of the sonnō jōi movement which became highly influential during the Bakumatsu period in the events leading to them Meiji restoration.

description: painting by Mochizuki Gyokusen
Japanese philosopher
description_wiki: Ninomiya Sontoku (二宮 尊徳; September 4, 1787 – November 17, 1856), also known as Ninomiya Kinjirō (二宮 金次郎), was a Japanese agriculturalist.

description: Sun goddess in Shinto
description_wiki: Amaterasu Ōmikami (天照大（御）神; Japanese pronunciation: [aꜜ.ma.te.ɾa.sɯ | oː.mʲiꜜ.ka.mʲi]), often called Amaterasu ([aꜜ.ma.te.ɾa.sɯ]) for short, also known as Amateru Kami (天照神) and Ōhirume no Muchi (大日孁貴), is the goddess of the sun in Japanese mythology.

description: third largest island of Japan
description_wiki: Kyushu (九州, Kyūshū; pronounced [kʲɯꜜː.ɕɯː] , lit.

description: Austronesian people of ancient Japan
description_wiki: The Kumaso (熊襲) were a prehistoric (semi-legendary) people of ancient Japan mentioned in the Kojiki, believed to have lived in the south of Kyūshū until at least the Nara period.

description: city in the Kyōto Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kyoto ( or ; Japanese: 京都, Kyōto [kʲoꜜː.to] ), officially Kyoto City (京都市, Kyōto-shi; [kʲoː.toꜜ.ɕi] ), is the capital city of Kyoto Prefecture in the Kansai region of Japan's largest and most populous island of Honshu.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: American naval officer (1794–1858)
designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: capital city of the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Victoria is the capital city of the Canadian province of British Columbia, located on the southern tip of Vancouver Island off Canada's Pacific coast.

description: largest city in the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Vancouver is a major city in Western Canada, located in the Lower Mainland region of British Columbia.

aliases: Great San Francisco Earthquake and Fire
description: major earthquake that struck San Francisco and the coast of Northern California
description_wiki: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake was a major earthquake which struck the coast of Northern California at 05:12 am Pacific Standard Time on Wednesday, April 18, 1906.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity
description_wiki: Methodism, also called the Methodist movement, is a Protestant Christian tradition whose origins, doctrines and practice derive from the life and teachings of John Wesley.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: city in California, United States
description_wiki: Livingston is a city in Merced County, California, United States.

description: unincorporated community in California
description_wiki: Cortez is an unincorporated community in Merced County, California.
